title: Iliad, Book 16: The Death of Patroclus
description: Book 16 of Homer's Iliad: The Death of Patroclus.
introduction: Book 16 of the Iliad: The Death of Patroclus. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Ὣς οἱ μὲν περὶ νηὸς ἐϋσσέλμοιο μάχοντο.
translation.text: Patroclus, weeping, begged Achilles to let him fight. Achilles lent him his armor and his Myrmidons, but warned him: drive the Trojans from the ships but come no further. Patroclus swept the Trojans back, killed Sarpedon, son of Zeus, and drove on toward Troy. But Apollo struck him from behind, Euphorbus stabbed him, and Hector killed him. Dying, Patroclus prophesied Hector's death.
